statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of two integers: n and k, separated by a space.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be binomial coefficient C(n, k) as an integer.

hints.text: The issue lies in handling the special cases when n or k is greater than the other.

editorial.title: Editorial for Incorrect Calculation of Binomial Coefficient
editorial.intuition: The correct approach involves handling the special cases when n or k is greater than the other.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive recursive approach or manual calculation
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof for the optimal solution
commonMistakes: Mistaking special cases for the general case
Failing to handle the special cases

starterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int solve(int n, int k) { int ans = 1; for (int i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i + 1) / i; } return ans; }};
starterCode.java: class Solution {public int solve(int n, int k) { int ans = 1; for (int i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i + 1) / i; } return ans; }};
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, n: int, k: int) -> int: ans = 1 for i in range(1, k + 1): ans = ans * (n - i + 1) // i return ans
starterCode.javascript: var solve = function(n, k) { var ans = 1; for (var i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i + 1) / i; } return ans; }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution {public: int solve(int n, int k) { int ans = 1; for (int i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i) / i; } return ans; }};
buggyStarterCode.java: class Solution {public int solve(int n, int k) { int ans = 1; for (int i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i) / i; } return ans; }};
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, n: int, k: int) -> int: ans = 1 for i in range(1, k + 1): ans = ans * (n - i) // i return ans
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(n, k) { var ans = 1; for (var i = 1; i <= k; i++) { ans = ans * (n - i) / i; } return ans; }

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, n: int, k: int) -> int:

solutions.python:
class Solution: def solve(self, n: int, k: int) -> int: if k > n - k: k = n - k ans = 1 for i in range(1, k + 1): ans = ans * (n - i + 1) // i return ans

if __name__ == '__main__': n, k = map(int, input().split()) print(Solution().solve(n, k))
